output:
CVE-2022-43526 describes multiple vulnerabilities within the web-based management interface of the Aruba EdgeConnect Enterprise Orchestrator. These vulnerabilities can allow a remote attacker to conduct reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, enabling them to execute arbitrary script code in the victim's browser. This vulnerability affects various versions of Aruba EdgeConnect Enterprise Orchestrator, including on-premises, as-a-Service, SP, and Global Enterprise Tenant Orchestrators up to certain versions.

The vulnerability is classified under CWE-79, which involves improper neutralization of user-controllable input before placing it in output used as a web page. The severity of this vulnerability is rated as MEDIUM. Successfully exploiting this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized execution of scripts, potentially compromising user data and credentials.

The vulnerability is associated with MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1189, which relates to the exploitation of public-facing applications. To mitigate these vulnerabilities, organizations are encouraged to apply recommended security updates and patches, sanitize user input, and employ web application firewalls (WAFs) to detect and block malicious activities.
